name: pain-trend-chart
title: Pain Trend Chart
description: Reported pain over time with analgesia marked. Each scoring instrument is its own series — scores from different scales are not comparable.

props.name: data
props.description: `{ time, score, scale }`. The scale is required on every reading.

props.name: analgesia
props.description: Doses aligned to the x axis.

props.name: reviewThreshold
props.description: Score at or above which the caller's protocol expects a review.

props.name: max
props.description: Highest score the scales run to.

clinicalNotes: The scale is part of the data, so each instrument is plotted as its own series: a 6 on the numeric rating scale and a 6 on FLACC are not the same observation, and joining them draws a trend nobody reported. This matters most exactly where it is easiest to get wrong — a child who becomes able to self-report moves from an observed scale to a self-reported one, and the apparent jump belongs to the instrument, not the pain. Pain is what the patient says it is: the chart does not smooth scores toward what the analgesia would predict, and a score that stays high after a dose is data, not noise.
notes: Pairs with the existing pain-scale input. Composes chart.
